SEARCH
Package com.comp2042.view.manager

Class GameModeUIManager

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.view.manager.GameModeUIManager
public class GameModeUIManager extends Object
Manages mode-specific UI components and visibility. Controls display of panels for different game modes and applies theme customizations. Uses UIHelper for common UI operations.
Author:
Dong, Jia

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    GameModeUIManager()
    Constructs a GameModeUIManager with default state.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameModeUIManager

      public GameModeUIManager()
      Constructs a GameModeUIManager with default state. UI components must be set via setter methods before use.

  • Method Details

    • setLeftObjectiveBox

      public void setLeftObjectiveBox(javafx.scene.layout.VBox leftObjectiveBox)
      Sets the level objective box component.
      Parameters:
      leftObjectiveBox - the level objective box

    • setStatisticsBox

      public void setStatisticsBox(javafx.scene.layout.VBox statisticsBox)
      Sets the statistics box component.
      Parameters:
      statisticsBox - the statistics box

    • setBestStatsBox

      public void setBestStatsBox(javafx.scene.layout.VBox bestStatsBox)
      Sets the best statistics box component.
      Parameters:
      bestStatsBox - the best statistics box

    • setHoldPanel

      public void setHoldPanel(javafx.scene.layout.GridPane holdPanel)
      Sets the hold panel component.
      Parameters:
      holdPanel - the hold panel

    • setNextBrickPanel

      public void setNextBrickPanel(javafx.scene.layout.GridPane nextBrickPanel)
      Sets the next brick panel component.
      Parameters:
      nextBrickPanel - the next brick panel

    • showLevelModeUI

      public void showLevelModeUI()
      Shows the Level Mode UI and hides other mode-specific UI. Sets up the level objective box and best statistics display.

    • hideLevelModeUI

      public void hideLevelModeUI()
      Hides the Level Mode UI. Resets level mode state and shows endless mode UI if applicable.

    • showEndlessModeUI

      public void showEndlessModeUI()
      Shows the Endless Mode UI. Displays statistics box and hides level-specific UI.

    • setEndlessMode

      public void setEndlessMode(boolean endlessMode)
      Sets the Endless Mode state.
      Parameters:
      endlessMode - true to enable endless mode

    • setTwoPlayerMode

      public void setTwoPlayerMode(boolean twoPlayerMode)
      Sets the Two-Player Mode state.
      Parameters:
      twoPlayerMode - true to enable two-player mode

    • resetToDefaultMode

      public void resetToDefaultMode()
      Resets all mode states to default (single-player endless mode).

    • applyThemeToPreviewDisplays

      public void applyThemeToPreviewDisplays(String accentColor)
      Applies theme colors to Hold and Next preview displays. Creates consistent styling with the current level theme.
      Parameters:
      accentColor - the theme's accent color (e.g., "#FFD700" for gold)

    • resetPreviewDisplayTheme

      public void resetPreviewDisplayTheme()
      Resets preview display styling to default theme.

    • isLevelMode

      public boolean isLevelMode()
      Checks if currently in Level Mode.
      Returns:
      true if in level mode

    • isEndlessMode

      public boolean isEndlessMode()
      Checks if currently in Endless Mode.
      Returns:
      true if in endless mode

    • isTwoPlayerMode

      public boolean isTwoPlayerMode()
      Checks if currently in Two-Player Mode.
      Returns:
      true if in two-player mode

    • getLevelStartTime

      public long getLevelStartTime()
      Gets the level start time in milliseconds.
      Returns:
      the level start time, or 0 if not in level mode

    • setLevelStartTime

      public void setLevelStartTime(long levelStartTime)
      Sets the level start time.
      Parameters:
      levelStartTime - the level start time in milliseconds
